question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the record stream downloader's isEnabled() returns false (e.g., via a public health/metrics endpoint) and time a staking-period-boundary transaction to exploit the gap in staking_reward_transfer ingestion, causing pending_reward to be permanently miscalculated and rewards to be stolen from other stakers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the setEnabled(false) code path in Downloader.verifySignatures() by crafting a stream file whose filename timestamp exceeds importerProperties.getEndDate(), causing the record downloader to stop ingesting staking reward transfers and leaving pending_reward permanently inflated for targeted accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a large getFrequency() polling interval on the record downloader to ensure that a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction crossing a UTC midnight boundary is not ingested before the next staking period begins, causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() to use a stale end_stake_period and compute an inflated pending_reward for the attacker's account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user, by observing the public /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards endpoint, detect that staking_reward_transfer records are not being ingested due to a high getFrequency() delay, and then submit a CryptoTransfer transaction at the exact staking period boundary to claim a double-counted pending_reward before the mirror node catches up?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfiguration where getStreamType() returns BALANCE instead of RECORD for the record downloader, causing staking_reward_transfer records to never be ingested, so that pending_reward in entity_stake is never reset after a payout and the attacker can query an inflated pending_reward value via the REST API?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isPersistBytes() is true, access the raw stream file bytes stored in the database (e.g., via a misconfigured read replica or exposed backup) to extract staking reward transfer amounts for all accounts before they are reflected in the public API, enabling front-running of reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isWriteFiles() is true and the archive destination folder is world-readable, read the written stream files from the filesystem to extract raw staking reward transfer data (account_id, amount, consensus_timestamp) before it is indexed, enabling targeted manipulation of staking positions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isWriteSignatures() is true and signature files are written to a predictable filesystem path, read the signature bytes to reconstruct which nodes signed which stream files, then identify periods where fewer than consensusRatio nodes participated, and exploit those gaps to claim staking rewards that were not properly validated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a consensusRatio of 0 in CommonDownloaderProperties (returned by getCommon()) to cause the ConsensusValidatorImpl to skip consensus checks entirely, allowing a single malicious node's stream file with fabricated staking_reward_transfer entries to be accepted and credited to the attacker's account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that getCommon().getDownloadRatio() is set below the consensusRatio threshold, causing the downloader to sample too few nodes and miss the NodeStakeUpdateTransaction that resets stake_period_start, resulting in pending_reward accumulating across multiple periods without being cleared?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the balance downloader's isEnabled() to be false (default is false per BalanceDownloaderProperties) and exploit the resulting absence of account_balance snapshots, causing EntityStakeRepository.updateEntityStake() to use stale stake_total_start values and compute inflated pending_reward for the attacker's staked account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated toggling of setEnabled() via a race condition in the CutoverService, causing the record downloader to skip processing of staking reward transfer records at a period boundary, leaving entity_stake.pending_reward uncorrected and allowing the attacker to query an inflated reward via /api/v1/accounts/{id}/rewards?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getFrequency() controls the @Scheduled polling interval of RecordFileDownloader.download(), and by observing the public mirror node API lag, identify a window where two staking reward payouts occur in the same block across a day boundary (as in twoStakingRewardPayoutsInSameBlockAcrossDayBoundary) but only one is ingested, causing stake_period_start to be set incorrectly and pending_reward to be double-counted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isPersistBytes() is false (default), exploit the fact that stream file bytes are nulled out after verification to prevent forensic detection of a tampered staking reward transfer record, allowing a manipulated pending_reward value to persist in entity_stake without evidence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a large getCommon().getTimeout() value to cause the Flux.timeout() in downloadAndParseSigFiles() to not fire, allowing a slow/malicious node to delay delivery of the NodeStakeUpdateTransaction stream file past the staking period boundary, causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to skip a period and leave pending_reward uncalculated for legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user, by querying the public /api/v1/accounts/0.0.800 endpoint and observing that pending_reward for the staking reward account is not being updated, infer that the record downloader's isEnabled() is false and exploit the stale entity_stake data to submit transactions that claim rewards based on an outdated stake_period_start?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a scenario where getStreamType() returns RECORD but the CutoverService switches to BLOCK stream mid-period, causing a gap in staking_reward_transfer ingestion at the cutover boundary and leaving pending_reward inflated for accounts that received rewards during the gap?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isWriteFiles() is true and the archive path is derived from importerProperties.getArchiveDestinationFolderPath(), predict the archive path and read written record stream files to extract staking reward transfer data (account_id, amount) for all accounts in a given period, enabling targeted staking manipulation before the data is publicly indexed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a small getCommon().getBatchSize() (default 25) to cause the downloader to process stream files in very small batches, creating a window where a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction is in a later batch than the staking reward transfers it should accompany, causing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to compute pending_reward using mismatched node_stake and staking_reward_transfer data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean enabled)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled(false) is called when endDate is exceeded in verifySignatures(), and by observing the public API to detect when the importer stops processing, identify a window where staking reward transfers for account 0.0.800 are not being recorded, causing pending_reward to remain inflated indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a very short getFrequency() (e.g., 500ms for record downloader) combined with a high-volume transaction flood to cause the downloader to process staking reward transfers out of order relative to the NodeStakeUpdateTransaction, causing the pending_reward calculation in EntityStakeRepository.updateEntityStake() to use the wrong epoch_day?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user, when isPersistBytes() is true, cause the database to store large stream file blobs that exhaust storage, triggering a database error during entity_stake update and leaving pending_reward in an inconsistent state that benefits the attacker's staked account?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit getCommon().getPathRefreshInterval() being very large, causing the downloader to use a stale node address book that excludes nodes which submitted the NodeStakeUpdateTransaction, so that the staking reward rate used in pending_reward calculation is from a previous period and rewards are systematically underpaid to legitimate stakers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BalanceDownloaderProperties.isEnabled() defaults to false, causing account_balance snapshots to never be ingested, so that EntityStakeRepository.createEntityStateStart() uses stale balance data and computes an inflated stake_total_start for the attacker's account, leading to a higher pending_reward?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a misconfigured getCommon().getMaxSize() that is too small to download a large record stream file containing many staking reward transfers, causing the download to fail silently and those staking_reward_transfer records to never be persisted, leaving pending_reward uncorrected for affected accounts?
